english_query: how we build a picture of the world from small pieces of what we see
ugf_response:
Person number one: How do we know what the world is like?

Person number two: We do not see everything at once. We only see small bits of things at a time.

Person number one: But if we only see small bits, how do we know how the whole thing looks?

Person number two: Our minds take those small bits and put them together. We use what we saw before to make a guess about what we see now.

Person number one: Is it like making a big picture from many tiny points of color?

Person number two: Yes, that is a good way to say it. Each tiny point is a small thing. When you put all those small things together, you see a whole picture.

Person number one: What if a small piece is wrong?

Person number two: Then the whole picture in our heads will be wrong too. We might think something is one way when it is actually another way.

Person number one: So our view of the world is just a big pile of small pieces put together?

Person number two: Yes, it is a pile that is not real. It is a guess that we make using the small bits we find.
